articles.title: Oil prices rise as US-Iran tensions disrupt shipping
articles.summary: Oil prices rise as US-Iran tensions disrupt shipping Oil prices rose after several days of tit-for-tat strikes between the United States and Iran disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and renewed concerns about global energy supplies. Brent crude futures gained 58 cents, or 0.8 percent, to $72.57 a barrel by 02:07 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate crude rose 88 cents, or 1.3 percent, to $70.11 a barrel. articles.summary: Israeli attacks have killed over 4,247 people in Lebanon since 2 March Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health says at least 4,247 people have been killed in Israeli attacks since 2 March, according to figures released by the ministry's Health Emergency Operations Center. The ministry added that 12,195 people have been wounded over the same period. articles.title: Washington and Tehran agree to halt strikes, US official says
articles.summary: Washington and Tehran agree to halt strikes, US official says The United States and Iran will temporarily halt strikes and allow ships to sail freely as technical negotiations on all aspects of the memorandum of understanding resume, a US official told Reuters. The development follows days of military exchanges between the two countries in and around the Strait of Hormuz. According to the official, technical talks are scheduled to restart on all items covered by the agreement as both sides seek to stabilise the situation and advance negotiations. articles.title: Kent on Reserve Bank of Australia's New Framework
articles.summary: Australian central bank Assistant Governor Chris Kent said the institution will be better prepared to respond to the next crisis it faces following a review of alternative monetary policy tools. In a speech in Sydney, Kent, who oversees financial markets at the Reserve Bank, said the cash rate target remains the primary and preferred instrument, but additional tools can play an important role during extraordinary times and provide some extra support. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
articles.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.summary: Report says Israel rebrands Gaza displacement plan as 'Freedom of Movement' Israel has renamed its controversial plan to encourage Palestinians to leave Gaza, dropping the term "voluntary migration" in favour of "Freedom of Movement Plan", Aljazeera reported, citing Israel's Channel 13. The report said Israeli security officials and the Mossad have been instructed to stop using the “voluntary migration”, which had drawn sharp international criticism.  Channel 13 said Israeli officials believe the new name could make foreign governments more willing to cooperate with the plan. articles.title: Gaza journalists work from tents after Israel destroys media offices
articles.summary: Gaza journalists work from tents after Israel destroys media offices Between 60 and 75 percent of journalists in Gaza have lost their homes or been forcibly displaced since October 7, 2023, according to the Freedoms Committee of the Palestinian Journalists Syndicate. In a report titled “Media without walls”, the committee said about 265 journalists have been killed since the start of Israel’s war on Gaza, making it one of the deadliest conflicts for media workers anywhere in the world. articles.title: US and Iran agree to resume Strait of Hormuz talks: Report
articles.summary: US and Iran agree to resume Strait of Hormuz talks: Report Iran and the United States have agreed to pause the latest fighting in the Gulf and return to talks over the Strait of Hormuz, Axios reports. The move could halt days of attacks and counterattacks that have pushed an interim peace agreement close to collapse. Axios, citing a senior US official, said the two sides are expected to meet in Qatar on Tuesday. articles.title: Israel shells Syrian village near occupied Golan Heights
articles.summary: Israel shells Syrian village near occupied Golan Heights Israeli forces have shelled a village in southern Syria, close to the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights, Syria’s official news agency SANA reports. SANA said Israeli artillery targeted Abidin, in the western countryside of Deraa province. The agency also reported Israeli military aircraft flying over areas of Quneitra and Deraa, as Israel continues to expand its military activity across southern Syria.
